issue_title: pypi page and docs should show changes
issue_body:
bleach has a `CHANGES` file which lists all the things that happened version-to-version. That's super. However, the contents of that file aren't available in the documentation on readthedocs nor on the PyPI page. This makes it difficult for people who are upgrading from one version of bleach to another to know what changed so they can figure out how it impacts them.

Further, we should be marking "backwards-incompatible" changes more obviously and explaining the old way and the new way.

These changes should be reflected in the release process. If there isn't one of those, then we should add that, too.

from elasticsearch_dsl import DocType, Search

This simple code cause `IndexError: list index out of range` ([full stack-trace](http://pastebin.com/D450xBUt))
```python

class TestEnum(enum.Enum):

s = [TestEnum.Test, TestEnum.Test]

```

Jsonpickle dumps first `TestEnum.Test` to `{"py/reduce": ...}` and second `TestEnum.Test` to `{"py/id": 1}`. But when jsonpickle decode first enum, it doesn't create reference to this object and it causes `IndexError` while parsing second enum.

issue_title: jsonpickle should more clearly warn about security hazard
issue_body:
For example, there exists a "safe" flag that tries to be safe. But does it protect against attacks? Not quite:

    >>> jsonpickle.unpickler.decode("""
    ...     {"py/object": "__builtin__.eval",
    ...     "py/initargs": {"py/tuple": ["__import__('os').system('sh')"]}}
    ... """, safe=True)
    sh-4.3$

or even simpler:

    >>> jsonpickle.unpickler.decode('{"py/reduce": [{"py/function": "os.system"},["sh"],0,0,0]}', safe=True)
    sh-4.3$ 

Currently the docs at https://jsonpickle.github.io/ only say (and only halfway down the page):

> Warning: Loading a JSON string from an untrusted source represents a potential security vulnerability. jsonpickle makes no attempt to sanitize the input.

This is far too weak. Loading a jsonpickle from an untrusted source is _always_ a security vulnerability.

Compare with the pickle docs, which are much clearer, and put this right at the very top of the page rather than below the fold:

> Warning: The pickle module is not secure against erroneous or maliciously constructed data. Never unpickle data received from an untrusted or unauthenticated source.

issue_title: Logg error handlers state
Avoid exception handling for PostgresTarget.create_marker_table() with "CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS"
issue_body:
I think it would be more elegant to add `IF NOT EXISTS` in the query for creating the `marker_table` in `luigi.contrib.postgres` module, instead of dealing with an exception to handle the case where the table already exists.

```sql
CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {marker_table} {
update_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
target_table TEXT,
inserted TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW())
```

`IF NOT EXISTS` is available since postgres 9.1 (released more than 5 years ago) and it seems that it is also available in many RDBMS.

So if the method should be rewrote I guess it would be as follows:

```python
    def create_marker_table(self):
        """
        Create marker table if it doesn't exist.

        Using a separate connection since the transaction might have to be reset.
        """
        connection = self.connect()
        connection.autocommit = True
        cursor = connection.cursor()
        if self.use_db_timestamps:
            sql = """ CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {marker_table} (
                      update_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                      target_table TEXT,
                      inserted TIMESTAMP DEFAULT NOW())
                """.format(marker_table=self.marker_table)
        else:
            sql = """ CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS {marker_table} (
                      update_id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
                      target_table TEXT,
                      inserted TIMESTAMP);
                  """.format(marker_table=self.marker_table)
        try:
            cursor.execute(sql)
        except psycopg2.ProgrammingError as e:
                # in case there is another exception, i.e. other than a duplicated table name, raise
                raise
        connection.close()
```

Again I am not experienced enough to make a PR, so if any good soul can manage to do it...

Thanks

issue_title: Please provide sane C API access to matrices and vectors in Assimp 4.0
issue_body:
Currently aiVector3D stores its data as ```{float x,y,z;}```. This is a little bit inconvenient, but acceptable. However, aiMatrix4x4 is stored as ```{	float a1,a2,a3,a4,b1,b2,b3,b4,c1,c2,c3,c4,d1,d2,d3,d4;}```, which is just ridiculous...

Please, please, please make that a float[3] and a float[4*4] and instead provide an indexing macro like ```#define AI_M4X4(m,i,j) (m[(i)*4+(j)])```, because it leads to insane code like devurandom/lua-assimp@e2d2194092115098d304ad987c38a509a134deba.

issue_title: DWG Fails
issue_body:
Hi I have this file that doesn't file an importer for it. Assimp fails on it.

[Wohnungstyp 14.dwg.zip](https://github.com/assimp/assimp/files/1759379/Wohnungstyp.14.dwg.zip)

issue_title: It would really useful to read all material parameters not just small subset of them
issue_body:
Assimp lose a huge amount of material information by forcing all materials into restricted material model (with diffuse texture, etc.).   For example an FBX exported with Vray materials attached (a very common situation) will store all the Vray material information. But during conversion will be lost and the materials will be empty.

Rather than (or as well as)  expanding the material model to support a specific, but still very restricted PBR implementation. It would be a more general solution to read ALL the material parameters and present them to the user to implement as they wish.

I've started the process in this branch. This stores all the FBX parameters as well as those the material model understand.  Unfortunately I've let it code rot a little behind ToT, and it only supports FBX. 
https://github.com/griffin2000/assimp/tree/FBXParameters

issue_body: We should pass 'null' as an allowed type if the type is nullable in `parse_value`.

issue_title: Hardcoded django-cache backend serializer
issue_body:
Is there a reason why the cache backend hardcodes its `serializer` to `pickle`?

I'm using this to cache results in redis, since django allows me to configure redis SSL support via the `rediss` connection string and I don't have another option in production with Heroku. Using my redis SSL connection string as the backend breaks.

Going this route is working, but now I'm stuck with the `pickle` serializer. Looking for a way to help improve support for this case and trying to understand first!

issue_title: Streaming Uploads?
issue_body:
Hey,

Sorry for treating this as a mailing list, I didn't see any other method for contact, so I went ahead and opened an issue.

I'm trying to use boto3 to upload files uploaded to PyPI to S3. The majority of these files will be < 60MB but a handful of them will be larger (up to a few hundred MB in size). I'm trying to figure out what the right interface to use to do this is. Right now, in PyPI we have a streaming upload from the client along with the expected MD5 hash of the entire file once it's been uploaded. I'm wondering if I can do something like:

```python

import hashlib

class HashingFileWrapper:

    def __init__(self, wrapped, md5_hash):
        self.wrapped = wrapped
        self.md5_hash = md5_hash
        self.hash_ctx = hashlib.md5()

    def read(self, *args, **kwargs):
        chunk = self.read(*args, **kwargs)
        self.hash_ctx.update(chunk)
        if not chunk:
            if self.hash_ctx.hexdigest() != self.md5_hash:
                raise ValueError("Hash Does Not Match")


my_s3_object.put(
    Body=HashingFileWrapper(file_like_object, md5_hash),
    ContentLength=file_size,
    ContentMD5=md5_hash,
)
```

Will that stream it up to S3 without buffering the whole file in memory? If not, is my only option to buffer the data to a temporary file and then use the ``my_s3_object.upload_file()`` interface?

issue_title: GlobalObject serialization incorrect?
issue_body:
```
from colander import GlobalObject, SchemaNode
import collections

sn = SchemaNode(typ=GlobalObject(collections))
ser = sn.serialize(collections.OrderedDict)
print ser
    'OrderedDict'
des = sn.deserialize(ser)
    Invalid: {'': u'The dotted name "OrderedDict" cannot be imported'}
ser = '.OrderedDict'
des = sn.deserialize(ser)
    collections.OrderedDict
```

To summarize, the serialization only returns the `__class__` of a type, without a '.' appended. To get the class from the deserialize function it needs to be given a dotted class name, in which case the behavior seems to be appropriate.

Am I mistaken in how serialize/deserialize are supposed to work in this situation? Please advise.

issue_title: ORDER BY query quantifies given order by string (which might be an alias) with a table name
issue_body:
**Example**
ORDER BY query using the alias without a join:
```
str(Query.from_(a).select(a.b, fn.Sum(a.c).as_('alias')).groupby(a.c).orderby('alias') )
=> 'SELECT "b",SUM("c") "alias" FROM "a" GROUP BY "c" ORDER BY "alias"'
```
ORDER BY query using the alias with a join:
```
str(Query.from_(a).join(b).on(a.b_id == b.id).select(a.foo, fn.Sum(a.int_column).as_('alias')).groupby(a.foo).orderby('alias') )
=> 'SELECT "a"."foo",SUM("a"."int_column") "alias" FROM "a" JOIN "b" ON "a"."b_id"="b"."id" GROUP BY "a"."foo" ORDER BY "a"."alias"'
```

**Issue**
The second example shows that when using a string in the `orderby` which might be an alias, is quantified with the FROM-table.

**Suggestion**
Don't quantify with the FROM-table in case of a string.
I think it's the coders responsibility to properly quantify, if necessary. The databases would complain for invalid SQL in case of ambiguous column names.

Let me know if there's a reason to quantify it that I don't see.

issue_title: Feature Statistics: exclude text features and dates
issue_body:
##### Expected behavior
It is probably hard to characterize text features with dispersion, min, and max (not clear what to show here) so I propose to exclude this types of features in the visualization.


##### Actual behavior
Text features are included, reported are NaN's for a min, max, and dispersion.

issue_title: 'PlotConfigWidget' object has no attribute 'button_close'
Add an option to pyannotate to dump raw annotations without trying to edit the file
issue_body: We've got an internal use case where lib2to3 can't parse the target file (oh, okay, it's pyxl :-). The info in type_info.json would still be useful. Maybe we can add a flag to just dump the annotations in a nicer format than having to read type_info.json by hand.

issue_title: Should BROWSER=safari be enabled in travis.yml?
issue_body:
As of commit `7930bb186f8f98c5777c2eeae67ba8865a85f408`, the contents of `travis.yml` is:

```
language: python
sudo: false
python:
  - "2.6"
  - "2.7"
install:
  #- pip install pep8 --use-mirrors
  #- pip install pyflakes --use-mirrors
  - pip install robotframework
  - pip install selenium
  - pip install decorator
  - pip install mockito
  #- "if [[ ${BROWSER} == 'ie' ]]; then pip install ie_only_pkg; fi"
before_script:
  - "export DISPLAY=:99.0"
  - "sh -e /etc/init.d/xvfb start"
script:
  - "firefox --version"
  - "python test/run_tests.py python $BROWSER --noncritical known_issue_-_travisci"
env:
  matrix:
  - BROWSER=firefox
  #- BROWSER=ie # will be added when we have completed SauceLabs CI
  #- BROWSER=safari # will be added after #247 merged
```

The last line is `#- BROWSER=safari # will be added after #247 merged`,  however PR #247 has been merged.  Should this like be commented out?

issue_title: Send User-Agent header with an SDK product token
issue_body:
Requests' default is

```python
>>> import requests
>>> requests.utils.default_user_agent()
'python-requests/2.5.3 CPython/3.4.2 Darwin/13.4.0'
```

The thing to do is prepend `mapbox-sdk-py/$version`.

issue_title: NCSS validate_query with no variables
issue_body:
```python
from siphon.ncss import NCSS
ncss = NCSS('http://thredds.ucar.edu/thredds/ncss/nws/metar/ncdecoded/Metar_Station_Data_fc.cdmr')
query = ncss.query()
ncss.validate_query(query)
```
returns
```set()```

Looks like the nice short-circuit behavior results in the return of the empty variable query--I think a call to `bool()` is in order.

issue_title: Translations + META isues
issue_body:
Hi. Not sure if it's a 'meta' issue, but it certainly breaks the app at this point.

Create the object.
```
meta = Meta(
    title=_('Andrey'),
)
```
Pass it into template context. Raises the error: *UnicodeDecodeError at / 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xd0 in position 0: ordinal not in range(128)*
```
# Error during template rendering
{% if meta.title %}{% og_prop 'title' meta.title %}{% endif %}
```

Thanks ahead.

issue_title: Question: best way to add custom fields to Post model?
issue_body: What is the best way to set something like a "post type" for individual posts? For example, if I would like some posts to render (very) differently. Templates can be overridden easily, but I don't see a (good) way of adding fields to post model.

issue_title: djangocms-installer : requirements.txt can't fully be reused (a dependency is not available with pip).
issue_body:
I've installed djangocms with the `djangocms-installer` pip package (using default choices).

Here is the output of `pip freeze` :

    dj-database-url==0.3.0
    Django==1.7.10
    django-appconf==1.0.1
    django-classy-tags==0.6.2
    django-cms==3.1.3

    djangocms-file==0.1
    djangocms-flash==0.2.0
    djangocms-googlemap==0.3
    djangocms-inherit==0.1
    djangocms-installer==0.8.0
    djangocms-link==1.7.0.dev1
    djangocms-picture==0.1
    djangocms-style==1.5
    djangocms-teaser==0.1
    djangocms-text-ckeditor==2.6.0
    djangocms-video==0.1
    html5lib==0.9999999
    Pillow==3.0.0
    pytz==2015.6
    six==1.10.0
    tzlocal==1.2
    wheel==0.24.0

`djangocms-link==1.7.0.dev1` can't be found by `pip` even if I try something like `pip install [--allow-external djangocms-link==1.7.0.dev1] [--allow-unverified djangocms-link==1.7.1.dev1] djangocms-link==1.7.0.dev1`.
It simply says :

    Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement djangocms-link==1.7.0.dev1 (from -r requirements.txt (line 17)) (from versions: 1.0, 1.0.1, 1.0.2, 1.0.3, 1.0.4, 1.0.5, 1.0.6a1, 1.1, 1.1.1, 1.1.2, 1.1.3, 1.2, 1.3, 1.3.1, 1.3.2, 1.3.3, 1.3.4, 1.3.5, 1.4.0, 1.4.1, 1.5, 1.6, 1.6.1, 1.6.2)
No matching distribution found for djangocms-link==1.7.0.dev1 (from -r requirements.txt (line 17))

Why doesn't djangocms-installer use packages available with pip?

How could I fix this? Could an other version do the job?

(I tried and It looks like using the github repository address for djangocms-link can do the job wouldn't it be better to use directly a version available on pip like for the other packages?)

I tried with python3.5 (But django1.7 isn't supported with this one), and python3.4.
Pip version is 7.1.2

I'll also quote @yakky that asked me to reopen here this issue I already opened in divio/django-cms.

"**TL;DR: installer uses github version of plugins in some cases, use dump mode (http://djangocms-installer.readthedocs.org/en/latest/usage.html#dump-mode) in case you want to reuse requirements**"

But if I want to run in my case `djangocms -p . projectname -R > requirements.txt` I get this :

    Path '.' already exists and is not empty, please choose a different one
    If you want to use this path anyway use the -s flag to skip this check.

So can't I create a requirements file with an already built project?

Thank you.

issue_title: Can not initialize an instance of the Encoder class
Server connection thread probably hangs
issue_body:
It's very difficult to reproduce this bug, but we've seen it happen with multiple computers connected to the robot wireless. My suspicion is that the `socket.settimeout` that is commented out in `SocketServerStreamProvider` is the culprit. Unfortunately, that doesn't work in python 2.7, so I'm loathe to uncomment it. However, I suspect nobody runs NetworkTable servers using python 2.7... 

The good news is that the robot code does *not* hang when this occurs -- it only disables networktables transmissions.

issue_title: pipenv is not usable for any project that has production-only dependencies
issue_body:
Hi, we're running into some issues that make pipenv (which I really like by the way), unfortunately unusable for us.

The current "dev-packages" and "packages" mechanism, while enough for many simple use cases, does not allow to express dependencies that are only available in production, be it because of system dependencies (e.g. picamera can't be installed on a mac or windows), hardware dependencies (GPU dependencies on a laptop without a GPU), or simply because we do not want that dependency in dev.

I know there are a number of closed issues that relate to this very problem, however I don't think any of them mentions packages that are production-only by design. This is a major hurdle and having a separate workflow for production seems to defeat the purpose of a package manager, at least for us.

Any chance a solution might be implemented in the short-term @kennethreitz ?

issue_title: Wheel caching on private index vs Pipfile.lock hashes
issue_body:
I'm trying to introduce version pinning for our company's python projects, but am having trouble adopting Pipenv to our needs and would welcome some guidance.                                                                               

We are using a private PyPI mirror (Artifactory remote repository) to cache all upstream packages.                                                                                                                                                                                       
Due to the fact that we use a libmusl based Linux distribution (Alpine), there are no matching binary wheels available on PyPI, requiring us to build a lot of stuff from source.                                                                                                         
To reduce build times we cache the built upstream packages by uploading them as wheels to our private PyPI mirror (Artifactory virtual repository). This is done automatically by our buildsystem.                                                                                        

From my current understanding, this seems to be incompatible with Pipfile.lock, because the hashes of these new wheels aren't available at the point in time that the Pipfile.lock was written.                                                                                           

Currently I see these choices:                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            
a) Use pipenv just for version pinning but disable hashing                                                                                                                                                                                                                                
b) Let the buildsystem commit a new Pipfile.lock to git every time it caches new upstream wheels                                                                                                                                                                                          
c) Expect the developers to manually upload built upstream wheels before running Pipfile.lock                                                                                                                                                                                             
d) Don't do upstream build caching                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        
e) Contribute toward a MUSL wheel variant for PyPI
f) ...?                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        

a) looks acceptable to me, but I haven't found a way to disable hashing / hash checking - is there anything I can provide in the Pipfile or the command line to do so?                                                                                                                    
If not, would you be willing to add something to that effect, or accept contributions for it?                                                                                                                                                                                             


Tangentially related: After reading through https://github.com/pypa/pipenv/issues/2159 am I correct in assuming that the [[source]]urls in the Pipfile are used like --extra-index-url and not as --index-url?                                                                            
If so, is it possible to force pipenv to not use https://pypi.python.org/simple?

It's my proposal. Assume that we in folder `~/dev/project/dummy`. let's create virtual environment with command and argument `pipenv shell --name=subname`. It should create path `~/.local/share/virtualenvs/dummy-subname`. Else it just uses old mechanism.

issue_title: pip skips fetching package from "install_requires" section
issue_body:
Hi,

I have a package (**testRecord1**) hosted on PyPi. I'm able to `pip install` the package on Linux platform. If I add this package in `install_requires` section of another package's setup.py and try to create a wheel for it, the wheel creation fails with the following : 
```
 Analyzing links from page https://pypi.python.org/simple/testrecord1/
    Skipping link https://pypi.python.org/packages/ec/d6/a70e8a40358e05a41269feecfcb231d0f42680ddddab9dcdbe911e6310f0/testRecord1-2018.0.0-py2.py3-none-manylinux1_x86_64.whl#md5=b440157247b4d09102ab450b70b4b3ce (from https://pypi.python.org/simple/testrecord1/); No binaries permitted for testRecord1
 Could not find a version that satisfies the requirement testRecord1 (from testRecord2==0.2) (from versions: )
```

Would really appreciate some feedback on how to circumvent this. I've also tried creating **testRecord1**'s wheel with :
```
class BinaryDistribution(dist.Distribution):
    def is_pure(self):
      return False

setup(...
distclass=BinaryDistribution
)
```
Still no luck.

Thanks,
Rohit

```
[bdist_wheel]
universal = 1
```
)

issue_title: api: Implement compose_objects just like minio-go
issue_body:
High-level CopyObject requirements:

- [x] Support copying objects of all sizes.
- [x] Support source objects with an arbitrary range header (i.e. any valid start and end offset of a source object) via multipart copy object.
- [x] Support all copy conditions (this is already supported).

Currently, the library supports only copying objects <= 5GiB in size. Larger objects can also be copied via a multipart-copy-object strategy.

The multipart copy object operation consists of starting a new multipart upload, followed by 1 or more copy-object-part requests, and finally a complete-multipart request.

Note that copy object via a single PUT request does not support range headers, but copy-object-part does support this.

This feature was recently implemented in the [Haskell SDK](https://github.com/minio/minio-hs/commit/e4e2576c7488878661eaa2d7fa68ab12e177d094).

issue_title: python minio client may cause thread exhaust
issue_body:
- python: 3.5
- minio-client: 2.2.6
- minio-server info: 
VERSION 2017-10-27T18:59:02Z
MEMORY
Used: 7.5 MB | Allocated: 50 GB | Used-Heap: 7.5 MB | Allocated-Heap: 29 MB
PLATFORM
Host: 6cbd0abfd258 | OS: linux | Arch: amd64
RUNTIME
Version: go1.9.2 | CPUs: 12

I use python script to create img and save record into db, and use minio python client upload to minio server.
```python
minio_client = Minio('192.168.1.144:9000',
                     access_key='',
                     secret_key='',
                     secure=False)


def upload(file_path, key_name):
  try:
    minio_client.fput_object('intellid',
                             key_name,
                             file_path,
                             content_type='image/png',
                             metadata={'Content-Disposition': 'attachment'})
  except ResponseError as err:
    logging.exception(file_path)
    logging.exception('upload failed!')


def main():
  main_id = threading.get_ident()
  db = default_session()
  gens = db.query(Generation).filter(Generation.status == None).limit(5).all()

  processed = 0
  while gens and len(gens) != 0:
    for gen in gens:
      compositions = copy.deepcopy(gen.composition['compositions'])
      for composition in compositions:
        composition['img_path'] = path.join(data_home, path.basename(
          db.query(Img).filter(Img.guid == composition[
            'guid']).first().oss))

      out_img = compose(gen.composition['width'], gen.composition['height'],
                        gen.composition['linkType'], compositions)

      key_name = 'ori/' + str(uuid.uuid4()) + '.png'
      key_name_thumb = 'thumb/' + str(uuid.uuid4()) + '.png'

      tmp = path.join(prod_out, key_name)
      thumb_tmp = path.join(prod_out, key_name_thumb)

      out_img.save(tmp)
      res = post_proc(tmp, thumb_tmp, 300)

      guid = uuid.uuid1().int >> 65
      while True:
        img = db.query(Img).filter(Img.guid == guid).first()
        if not img:
          break
        guid = uuid.uuid1().int >> 65
      minio_oss = minio_oss_prefix + key_name
      minio_oss_thumb = minio_oss_prefix + key_name_thumb

      new_img = Img(guid=guid, oss=minio_oss, thumb_oss=minio_oss_thumb,
                    file_type='PNG', img_type='AI', attr=res)
      print("before upload acitve_thread {}.".format(threading.active_count()))
      upload(tmp, key_name)
      upload(thumb_tmp, key_name_thumb)
      print("after upload acitve_thread {}.".format(threading.active_count()))
      db.add(new_img)
      db.commit()
      gen.guid = guid
      gen.status = 0
      processed += 1
      db.commit()
      print("processed {}.".format(processed))
    # end for
    gens = db.query(Generation).filter(Generation.status == None).limit(
      5).all()
  # end while


if __name__ == '__main__':
  main()

```
> partial log print:
before upload acitve_thread 319.
after upload acitve_thread 322.
processed 107.
before upload acitve_thread 322.
after upload acitve_thread 325.
processed 108.
before upload acitve_thread 325.
after upload acitve_thread 328.
processed 109.


**problem:** the minio client will create `ThreadPool` with 3 thread (code location: minio/api.py line 1511). And these thread (minio.thread_pool.Worker) will nerver exit. After every iteration in loop, the active thread number will increase 3. Finally the total threads in current process will exceed the linux limit(2048 in `ulimit -a`).

I'm a novice in minio. appreciation for any help. thanks.

issue_title: Django admin filter is broken for enum fields
issue_body:
The url that django generates for filers (`field__exact=x`) ends up using `str(some_enum)` instead of `some_enum.value`  for `x`.

This is can be tested by adding a `field_x` (which is an enum field) to the `filters = ('field_x',)` of a registered model admin.

issue_body: Filenames with non-UTF-8 characters trigger a UnicodeDecodeError: ``` ~/workspace/pset4/recover/ $ submit50 cs50/2018/x/recover --verbose Connecting... Authenticating... git config --global credential.https://github.com/submit50.username git credential-cache --socket /home/ubuntu/.git-credential-cache/submit50 exit git -c credential.helper='cache --socket /home/ubuntu/.git-credential-cache/submit50 --timeout 604800' -c credentialcache.ignoresighup=true credential approve Preparing... git clone --bare https://username@github.com/submit50/username /tmp/tmp2o8vlje5 Cloning into bare repository '/tmp/tmp2o8vlje5'... Password for 'https://username@github.com': remote: Counting objects: 220, done. remote: Compressing objects: 100% (2/2), done. remote: Total 220 (delta 0), reused 3 (delta 0), pack-reused 217 Receiving objects: 100% (220/220), 5.24 MiB | 5.09 MiB/s, done. Resolving deltas: 100% (87/87), done. git checkout --force cs50/2018/x/recover .gitattributes error: pathspec '.gitattributes' did not match any file(s) known to git. git config user.email username@users.noreply.github.com git config user.name username git symbolic-ref HEAD refs/heads/cs50/2018/x/recover git config core.excludesFile /tmp/tmpyxoz26th git add --all git ls-files recover.c git ls-files --exclude-from=/tmp/tmp2o8vlje5/info/exclude --other "AWA\211\377AVI\211\366AUI\211\325ATL\215%\200\361 " card.raw recover Traceback (most recent call last): File "/opt/pyenv/versions/3.6.0/bin/submit50", line 11, in load_entry_point('submit50==2.4.7', 'console_scripts', 'submit50')() File "/opt/pyenv/versions/3.6.0/lib/python3.6/site-packages/submit50.py", line 128, in main submit("submit50", args["slug"]) File "/opt/pyenv/versions/3.6.0/lib/python3.6/site-packages/submit50.py", line 581, in submit others = [unescape(other) for other in others] File "/opt/pyenv/versions/3.6.0/lib/python3.6/site-packages/submit50.py", line 581, in others = [unescape(other) for other in others] File "/opt/pyenv/versions/3.6.0/lib/python3.6/site-packages/submit50.py", line 577, in unescape .decode("utf8") UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0x89 in position 3: invalid start byte Sorry, something's wrong! Let sysadmins@cs50.harvard.edu know! git credential-cache --socket /home/ubuntu/.git-credential-cache/submit50 exit Submission cancelled. ``` Reproduce with `python3` at the command line with: ``` >>> s = "AWA\211\377AVI\211\366AUI\211\325ATL\215%\200\361 " >>> s.encode('latin1').decode('unicode-escape').encode('latin1').decode('utf8') Traceback (most recent call last): File "", line 1, in UnicodeDecodeError: 'utf-8' codec can't decode byte 0x89 in position 3: invalid start byte ``` Results from https://github.com/cs50/submit50/blob/develop/submit50.py#L564-L581, which is inspired by https://stackoverflow.com/a/46650050/5156190. Goal of those lines is to display the filenames, when summarizing files to be submitted and not submitted, just as the students see them in their directory (as via `ls`), so that they recognize them: I.e., as: Instead of as: We could pass `-z` to `git-ls-files`, to avoid `git`'s escaping of Unicode characters. But then [`spawnu`](http://pexpect.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api/pexpect.html#handling-unicode) tends to choke, since it needs to know the encoding of the characters it's seeing (and doesn't like unrecognized encodings, as might happen with accidentally created filenames). Perhaps worth noting is how `zip` and `tar` handle this anomalous filename: ``` ~/workspace/pset4/ $ zip -r recover.zip recover/ adding: recover/ (stored 0%) adding: recover/recover.c (deflated 61%) adding: recover/recover (deflated 62%) adding: recover/AWAAVIAUITL% (stored 0%) ~/workspace/pset4/ $ tar cvf recover.tar recover recover/ recover/recover.c recover/recover recover/AWA\211\377AVI\211\366AUI\211\325ATL\215%\200\361 ```

issue_title: threshold_li Modifies Passed data
issue_body:
When calling `skimage.features.threshold_li(image)` the image is modified. The code most likely at fault is line 342:

```python
image -= offset
```

An easy workaround is to simply call `skimage.features.threshold_li(image.copy())` so that only a copy of the image is passed to the function. And the original data is left unmodified.

issue_title: make compare_psnr, compare_ssim, etc less flexible picky about dtypes and range
issue_body:
## Description

As mentioned previously in #2878, functions from `skimage.metrics.compare_psnr` fail when the input dtypes do not match.  This is a nuisance in cases where for example one array may be float32 and the other float64.  A specific example of this is that `denoise_nl_means` from releases <=0.13 return a float32 denoised image for float64 input.  The user must then convert to matching dtypes before PSNR, SSIM, etc can be computed.

It would be nice to remove this restriction.  I think some of these also restrict floats to lie in the range [0, 1] or [-1, 1] when this should also not be necessary.

This is something I will try to make a PR to resolve, but please provide feedback if there is a reason this should not be done.

issue_title: skimage.morphology functions should support all parameters of underlying scipy calls
issue_body:
For example, `binary_erosion` in [scipy](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy-0.16.1/reference/generated/scipy.ndimage.morphology.binary_erosion.html#scipy.ndimage.morphology.binary_erosion) and in [skimage](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/morphology/binary.py#L12) , `gray_closing` in [scipy](https://docs.scipy.org/doc/scipy-0.16.0/reference/generated/scipy.ndimage.morphology.grey_closing.html#scipy.ndimage.morphology.grey_closing) and in [skimage](https://github.com/scikit-image/scikit-image/blob/master/skimage/morphology/grey.py#L307).

It might be better to consider further changes in `scipy` functions' interfaces and refactor `skimage` functions using `*kwargs`.

issue_title: Add pycapnp to requirements.txt?
Provide source install for C++ dependencies
issue_body:
related to #593 

Currently most dependencies are prebuilt and the binaries are included in the nupic.core repo. This requires us to rebuild the libraries for each platform which can be error prone and bloat the repo. It also means that most platforms simply aren't supported.

This task is proposing that we use CMake ExternalProject functionality to fetch a specific source version of each C++ dependency from external repos. This should allow nupic.core to be built on a broad range of platforms and it will make it easier to maintain dependencies and update versions. We can add a caching layer to our CI system to reduce build times if that is problematic.

issue_body:

With the maturity of the Windows changes appearing in the core, via #275, this work-around needs undoing or reworking for all platforms.

issue_title: Provide a way to override or extend the Statsd module
issue_body:
It would be nice if consumers of cliquet could override or wrap the statsd module so they can use a different statsd implementation (such as datadog) or inject their own metadata into statsd calls.

For example, in my use case, we utilize datadog to track our metrics, which is compliant with the statsd protocol, but extends the API to introduce the concept of "tags". My team uses tags to inject extra metadata into metrics, such as values pulled in from custom internal HTTP headers. 

It would be nice if I could modify the statsd module to utlize datadog and extend the logic to automatically inject other metadata into the metric.

issue_title: bootstrap_jquery_url template tag calls slim version of jQuery
issue_body:
The template tag `{% bootstrap_jquery_url %}` currently calls the **slim** version of jQuery, which doesn't support quite a few features of the full jQuery version. See here:

https://github.com/zostera/django-bootstrap4/blob/master/bootstrap4/bootstrap.py#L19

I'm happy to issue a P.R. to include the full version of jQuery, but it raises the question of which direction to head:

Option (1): make the existing template tag point to the full version.
Option (2): make the existing template tag point to the full version, and add another template tag called `{% bootstrap_jquery_slim_url %}`
Option (3): add a template tag called `{% bootstrap_jquery_full_url %}` that points to the full version.

`django-bootstrap3` currently points to the full version of jQuery.

Thoughts?

Steps to reproduce:

issue_title: tests: simplify doctest execution
issue_body:
The following cookiecutter change:

   https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98

should be propagated to this Invenio module.

Namely, in `run-tests.sh`, the sphinx for doctests is invoked after pytest run:

```console
$ tail -3 ./\{\{\ cookiecutter.project_shortname\ \}\}/run-tests.sh
sphinx-build -qnNW docs docs/_build/html && python setup.py test && sphinx-build -qnNW -b doctest docs docs/_build/doctest
```

This sometimes led to problems on Travis CI with the second sphinx-build run due
to "disappearing" dependencies after the example application was tested.

A solution that worked for invenio-marc21 (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49#issuecomment-281287103)
and that was integrated in cookiecutter (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/cookiecutter-invenio-module/pull/98) was to
run doctest execution in pytest, removing the second `sphinx-build` invocation.

This both solved Travis CI build failures and simplified test suite execution.

Note that this change may necessitate to amend the code tests etc so that things
would be executed with the Flask application context (see
https://github.com/inveniosoftware/invenio-marc21/pull/49/commits/09e98fc18fd5591dc65d806286e8da51b7e384b4).

issue_title: Create a nicer UI for importing
issue_body:
This is related to pull request #16, which already updates the Import tab in the ZMI.

Perceived problem: the Import tab is too complicated.

Initial idea:
- Move the tarball import to a separate tab. Any checkboxes or other options on the current Import tab have no influence at all on importing a tarball. Tab name: Tarball or Archive.
- The rest of the Import tab can stay. The changes I did to the UI in pull request #16 can probably be reverted for this tab. Maybe remove 'Import all steps' and instead have a 'select/deselect steps' button/checkbox, although that might mean adding some javascript, which is currently totally not in use. Rename the 'Include dependencies?' checkbox to 'Include dependencies of selected steps?', because that is what this checkbox does. It has nothing to do with dependency profiles, if you ever thought that. In other words, the Import tab is for when you want to have full control over which steps are imported.
- Create a new tab, say titled Addons. Start with a list of profiles that have upgrade steps that can be run. Then have a list of profiles that can be applied. Add the new UI options from pull request #16 here, giving control over how dependency profiles are handled. Maybe we can actually show the list of dependency profiles and give their current status: installed / not installed / needs upgrade. Do not give the option to select individual import steps: this page is for importing a full profile.

Given the new Addons tab, it probably makes sense for the Import tab to not apply or upgrade any dependency profiles. We could warn when we see this is a problem, and point to the Addons tab.

issue_title: Multiple issues with latest Android SDK
issue_body:
I tried to compile an APK using python-for-android for the first time, using the Kivy Virtual Machine.

I have updated python-for-android to the latest github version ([commit 8829312fd187121384939627ec09354657821ae9](https://github.com/kivy/python-for-android/commit/8829312fd187121384939627ec09354657821ae9)) to avoid the ["Invalid or unsupported command list" error](https://github.com/kivy/python-for-android/issues/1070).

Since then, I faced two additional issues:

1. Build fail at `ant` call because `android-sdk/tools/ant/build.xml` does not exist anymore in latest releases of Android SDK. The only workaround I have found was to install an older SDK version prior to this change, such as android-sdk-r25.2.5. See [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/42912824/the-ant-folder-is-suddenly-missing-from-android-sdk-did-google-remove-it), [here](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/43648201/cannot-find-build-xml-error-building-android-sdk-sample) and [here](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/python-android/2fLGqp6Bcec).

Example error:
```
BUILD FAILED	
/home/kivy/.local/share/python-for-android/dists/unnamed_dist_2/build.xml:93: Cannot find /home/kivy/android/android-sdk/tools/ant/build.xml imported from /home/kivy/.local/share/python-for-android/dists/unnamed_dist_2/build.xml	
```

2. The build of Java classes is done with enforcement of v1.5 (-source 1.5), but `kivy/android/PythonService.java` includes a multi-catch statement that works only from v1.7 onwards.

Example error:
```
[INFO]:    STDOUT (last 20 lines of 79):                                       
		
-pre-compile:	
	
-compile:	
    [javac] Compiling 22 source files to /home/kivy/.local/share/python-for-android/dists/unnamed_dist_1/bin/classes	
    [javac] warning: [options] source value 1.5 is obsolete and will be removed in a future release	
    [javac] warning: [options] target value 1.5 is obsolete and will be removed in a future release	
    [javac] warning: [options] To suppress warnings about obsolete options, use -Xlint:-options.	
    [javac] /home/kivy/.local/share/python-for-android/dists/unnamed_dist_1/tmp-src/org/kivy/android/PythonService.java:107: error: multi-catch statement is not supported in -source 1.5	
    [javac]             } catch (NoSuchMethodException | IllegalAccessException |	
    [javac]                                            ^	
    [javac]   (use -source 7 or higher to enable multi-catch statement)	
    [javac] 1 error	
    [javac] 3 warnings	
	
BUILD FAILED	
/home/kivy/android/android-sdk-r25.2.5/tools/ant/build.xml:716: The following error occurred while executing this line:	
/home/kivy/android/android-sdk-r25.2.5/tools/ant/build.xml:730: Compile failed; see the compiler error output for details.	
```

I'm stuck at this point, as I could not locate where the v1.5 enforcement takes place.

issue_title: Version 0.5.0 expands buffers on OSX
issue_body:
I'm trying to get Brotli integrated into the backup program I'm developing, HashBackup.  Previously I had been testing with a version downloaded May 18th, and it worked fine on OSX and Linux.

I downloaded the latest version today for testing.  Instead of compressing, it is apparently expanding.  Any clues?

```
$ py
Python 2.7.9 (default, Jun 22 2016, 12:43:12) 
[GCC 4.2.1 (Apple Inc. build 5666) (dot 3)] on darwin
Type "help", "copyright", "credits" or "license" for more information.
>>> import brotli
>>> x=1024*' '
>>> z=brotli.compress(x)
>>> len(z)
11520
>>> x2=brotli.decompress(z)
>>> x==x2
True
>>> 
```

Here's a test with bro.py:

```
[jim@mb python]$ py bro.py -i x.bro -o x.cc -d
[jim@mb python]$ ls -l
total 120
-rw-r--r--@ 1 jim  staff    200 Jun 21 07:41 README.md
-rwxr-xr-x@ 1 jim  staff   5440 Jun 21 07:41 bro.py
-rw-r--r--@ 1 jim  staff   9048 Jun 21 07:41 brotlimodule.cc
drwxr-xr-x  6 jim  staff    204 Jun 21 07:41 tests
-rw-r--r--  1 jim  staff  21550 Jun 22 13:12 x.bro
-rw-r--r--  1 jim  staff   9048 Jun 22 13:12 x.cc
[jim@mb python]$ cmp x.cc brotlimodule.cc
```

Here's a buffer test:
```
>>> x='jim' + 100*' ' + 'my'
>>> z=brotli.compress(x)
>>> len(z)
10371
>>> z
'\x1bh\x00\x00\x8c\x94n\xe6\xa2lS\x03\xa9\xb4\xbc\x92\x04\xb4@\xf6\x89ree\x00_BrotliBuildHistogramsWithContext\x00_BrotliEstimateBitCostsForLiterals
(a bunch more garbage)
\x00_BrotliEncoderInputBlockSize\x00_BrotliEncoderWriteData\x00enc/././metablock.h\x00_BrotliEncoderCompressStream\x00_BrotliEncoderCompress\x00_BrotliEncoder'
>>> z=z[:200]
>>> brotli.decompress(z)
'jim                                                                                                    my'
>>> len(z)
200
>>> z=z[:100]
>>> brotli.decompress(z)
'jim                                                                                                    my'
>>> z=z[:50]
>>> brotli.decompress(z)
'jim                                                                                                    my'
>>> z
'\x1bh\x00\x00\x8c\x94n\xe6\xa2lS\x03\xa9\xb4\xbc\x92\x04\xb4@\xf6\x89ree\x00_BrotliBuildHistogramsWit'
>>> brotli.decompress(z[:25])
'jim                                                                                                    my'
>>> brotli.decompress(z[:13])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
brotli.error: BrotliDecompress failed
>>> brotli.decompress(z[:20])
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
brotli.error: BrotliDecompress failed
>>> brotli.decompress(z[:21])
'jim                                                                                                    my'
>>> 
```

So it appears to be compressing into a temp buffer but not setting the length right.  Probably a bug in python/brotlimodule.cc

issue_title: Release the Python GIL when compressing/decompressing
issue_body: The C extension doesn't currently release the GIL during [`compress_stream()`](https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/master/python/_brotli.cc#L92) and during `decompress_stream()` (between https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/master/python/_brotli.cc#L433 and https://github.com/google/brotli/blob/master/python/_brotli.cc#L452 which is a shame because we can enable greater parallelism .

issue_title: libbrotliencso and libbrotlidec.so should both require libbrotlicommon.so
issue_body:
both libbrotlidec and libbrotlienc use BrotliGetDictionary from libbrotlicommon, but rely on the binary using brotli to also load libbrotlicommon. It may be more convenient for end users to link the enc and dec libraries against libbrotlicommon.so. The mod_brotli included in apache httpd 2.4.29 does not automatically pick up this dependency, and as a consequence, build a mod_brotli that can only be used by LD_PRELOADind libbrotlicommon.so. 

Proposed patch:
```--- Makefile.am 2018-02-02 14:04:26.544647322 +0100
+++ ../brotli-1.0.2-autoconf/Makefile.am        2018-02-02 13:51:24.773377900 +0100
@@ -22,9 +22,9 @@
 libbrotlicommon_la_SOURCES = $(BROTLI_COMMON_C) $(BROTLI_COMMON_H)
 libbrotlicommon_la_LDFLAGS = $(AM_LDFLAGS) $(LIBBROTLI_VERSION_INFO) $(LDFLAGS)
 libbrotlidec_la_SOURCES = $(BROTLI_DEC_C) $(BROTLI_DEC_H)
-libbrotlidec_la_LDFLAGS = $(AM_LDFLAGS) $(LIBBROTLI_VERSION_INFO) $(LDFLAGS) -lbrotlicommon
+libbrotlidec_la_LDFLAGS = $(AM_LDFLAGS) $(LIBBROTLI_VERSION_INFO) $(LDFLAGS)
 libbrotlienc_la_SOURCES = $(BROTLI_ENC_C) $(BROTLI_ENC_H)
-libbrotlienc_la_LDFLAGS = $(AM_LDFLAGS) $(LIBBROTLI_VERSION_INFO) $(LDFLAGS) -lbrotlicommon
+libbrotlienc_la_LDFLAGS = $(AM_LDFLAGS) $(LIBBROTLI_VERSION_INFO) $(LDFLAGS)
 
 pkgconfigdir = $(libdir)/pkgconfig
 pkgconfig_DATA = \
```

mean_ret_spread_quant, std_spread_quant = perf.compute_mean_returns_spread(
        mean_compret_quant_daily,
        non_predictive_factor_data['factor_quantile'].max(),
        non_predictive_factor_data['factor_quantile'].min(),
        std_err=compstd_quant_daily)
fr_cols = len(factor_returns.columns)
class GridFigure(object):
    def __init__(self, rows, cols):
        self.rows = rows
        self.cols = cols
        self.fig = plt.figure(figsize=(14, rows * 7))
        self.gs = gridspec.GridSpec(rows, cols, wspace=0.4, hspace=0.3)
        self.curr_row = 0
        self.curr_col = 0

    def next_row(self):
        if self.curr_col != 0:
            self.curr_row += 1
            self.curr_col = 0
        subplt = plt.subplot(self.gs[self.curr_row, :])
        self.curr_row += 1
        return subplt

    def next_cell(self):
        if self.curr_col >= self.cols:
            self.curr_row += 1
            self.curr_col = 0
        subplt = plt.subplot(self.gs[self.curr_row, self.curr_col])
        self.curr_col += 1
        return subplt
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec
vertical_sections = 2 + fr_cols * 3
gf = GridFigure(rows=vertical_sections, cols=1)
ft.plotting.plot_quantile_statistics_table(non_predictive_factor_data)
ft.plotting.plot_returns_table(alpha_beta, mean_compret_quantile, mean_ret_spread_quant)
turnover_periods = ft.utils.get_forward_returns_columns(non_predictive_factor_data.columns)
quantile_factor = non_predictive_factor_data['factor_quantile']
quantile_turnover = {p: pd.concat([perf.quantile_turnover(
    quantile_factor, q, p) for q in range(1, int(quantile_factor.max()) + 1)],
                                        axis=1)
                            for p in turnover_periods}
autocorrelation = pd.concat(
        [perf.factor_rank_autocorrelation(non_predictive_factor_data, period) for period in
         turnover_periods], axis=1)
ft.plotting.plot_turnover_table(autocorrelation, quantile_turnover)
ft.plotting.plot_quantile_returns_bar(mean_compret_quantile,
                                       by_group=False,
                                       ylim_percentiles=None,
                                       ax=gf.next_row())
plt.show()

issue_title: update readme
5.4.2 not compatible with python 3
issue_body: `basestring` was removed from python 3. For python 3, you need to compare it to `str`.

issue_title: Consistency Namespace vs. ClosedNamespace
issue_body:
As I understand rdflib.Namespace and ClosedNamespace, they are both meant as auxiliaries to simplify the creation of URIRefs in Python code, e.g. `RDF.type` or `OWL.Class`. In addition, ClosedNamespace provides an additional vocabulary check, e.g., `RDF.spam` will raise an exception.

However, sometimes other uses of Namespace/ClosedNamespace can be useful, e.g., string comparison. At this stage, it is quite inconvenient that Namespace is derived from unicode, whereas ClosedNamespace is derived from the standard object, and the actual namespace string is an instance attribute (uri). In the interest of consistency, I propose that ClosedNamespace should also be derived from unicode (or even from Namespace).

Regards

issue_title: Namespace prefix not used in relative URI
issue_body:
Hi, I'm using RDFLib version 4.2.1. The following code snippet 
from rdflib import Graph, Literal
from rdflib.namespace import Namespace, RDFS
BASE=Namespace('http://example.com/')

g = Graph()
g.bind('', BASE)
g.add( (BASE.term('customer'), RDFS.label, Literal('Customer', lang='en')) )
g.add( (BASE.term('customer/1'), RDFS.label, Literal('Customer 1', lang='en')) )
print(g.serialize(format='turtle'))
results in two triples @prefix : . @prefix rdf: . @prefix rdfs: . @prefix xml: . @prefix xsd: . :customer rdfs:label "Customer"@en . rdfs:label "Customer 1"@en . In the last line I would expect a shorter version with _:customer/1_ subject. B.t.w. using the _base_ parameter in _serialize()_ does not seem to work (@base missing from namespace). Any suggestions?

issue_title: date serialization failure
issue_body:
The following code:

``` python
import rdflib
g = rdflib.Graph()
y = ' <http://a.example/X> <http://a.example/p> "2016-01-01T00:00:00"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime>,' \
    '                                           "2016-01-01T00:00:00Z"^^<http://www.w3.org/2001/XMLSchema#dateTime> .'

p=g.parse(data=y, format="turtle")
print(p.serialize(format="turtle").decode('utf-8'))
```

Causes an error:

```
  File "...lib/python3.5/site-packages/rdflib/term.py", line 822, in __gt__
    return self.value > other.value
TypeError: can't compare offset-naive and offset-aware datetimes
```

This is because the serializer is trying to sort the output tuples and the two different forms of date/time can't be compared.

Recommend the following change - line 822, term.py

``` python
            if self.value != None and other.value != None:
                try:
                    return self.value > other.value
                except TypeError:
                    return str(self.value) > str(other.value)
```

The idea being that, if the native comparison doesn't work, fall back on the string comparison.

issue_title: Could not load library ".../libpqwalreceiver.so"
issue_body:
We compile and install Postgres in a non-standard location (/opt/custom/share/postgresql-9.6.2_7 in the example below) which requires that we specify the `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` env var when starting up postgres and when working with postgres utilities. When starting up Postgres, Patroni currently passes along `PATH`, `LC_ALL` and `LANG` env vars if they are set, but does not do the same with `LD_LIBRARY_PATH`. This results in the following FATAL replication error on the replica node:
```
2018-06-05 21:10:26,675 INFO: Lock owner: postgresdb-01; I am postgresdb-02
2018-06-05 21:10:26,675 INFO: does not have lock
2018-06-05 21:10:26,676 INFO: no action.  i am a secondary and i am following a leader
FATAL:  could not load library "/opt/custom/share/postgresql-9.6.2_7/lib/libpqwalreceiver.so": libpq.so.5: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
FATAL:  could not load library "/opt/custom/share/postgresql-9.6.2_7/lib/libpqwalreceiver.so": libpq.so.5: cannot open shared object file: No such file or directory
```

The fix for this is to pass `LD_LIBRARY_PATH` along with the other env vars, which results in replication behaving as expected:
```
2018-06-05 22:03:08,722 INFO: Lock owner: postgresdb-01; I am postgresdb-02
2018-06-05 22:03:08,722 INFO: does not have lock
2018-06-05 22:03:08,727 DEBUG: Sending request(xid=49): SetData(path='/service/postgres_cluster/members/postgresdb-02', data='{"conn_url":"postgres://postgresdb-02:5432/postgres","api_url":"http://postgresdb-02:8008/patroni","timeline":1,"state":"running","role":"replica","xlog_location":184664496}', version=-1)
2018-06-05 22:03:08,737 DEBUG: Received response(xid=49): ZnodeStat(czxid=4294967413, mzxid=4294967497, ctime=1528235848173, mtime=1528236188727, version=28, cversion=0, aversion=0, ephemeralOwner=216194533074731009, dataLength=169, numChildren=0, pzxid=4294967413)
2018-06-05 22:03:08,741 INFO: no action.  i am a secondary and i am following a leader
```

I will submit a pull request shortly that addresses this problem.

issue_title: Do not crash when asked for a parameter after end date
issue_body:
As someone who use openfisca as a backend of a simulation application,
My application doesn't crash overnight without me having done anything,
Because we just passed the end date of one legislative parameter

----------------

The changes introduced with the fuzzy parameters are a catastrophe for anyone using openfisca in prod. It introduces a major production risk : if we pass the end date of a parameter we use, then the application cannot do any calculation anymore.

The PNDS (institutional re-user) application was down for some time because of this, as well as some of our testing instances.

Openfisca should offer a "production" mode where it is flexible and extrapolates parameters values, and a "strict" mode where it crashed when a value is missing if it is necessary.

issue_title: Add the possibility to evalueate Range values to calculate parameters using variables
issue_body:
Hi there!

I really enjoy OpenFisca, but I recently encountered an issue.

### Here is what I did:
Defining a parameter that depends on a variable value. 
This is the **parameter:** 
```
<=70:
description: Threshold within the retirement income is not taxable for people whom has an age major lower than 70  
unit: currency
  values:
    2017-01-01:
      value: 8000.00
70<:
  description: Threshold within the retirement income is not taxable for people whom has an age major than 70
  unit: currency
  values:
    2017-01-01:
      value: 7500.00
``` 
and this is the **variable** containing the formula that use this parameter.
``` 
class threshold_retirement_income_not_taxable(Variable):
    value_type = float
    entity = person
    definition_period = MONTH
    set_input = set_input_divide_by_period 
    label = "Income not taxable depending on person age"
    reference = "..."  

    def formula(person,period,parameters):
        age= person('age',period)
        threshold= parameters(period).taxes.IRPEF.income_not_taxable.threshold_retirement[eta]
        return threshold
``` 

### Here is what I expected to happen:
I expected that, this formula return me the value 8000 for people whom has an age less than 70 and the value 7500 for people whom has an age greater than 70. 

### Here is what actually happened:
This is not supported because **parameters using variables** only supports fixed values and not ranges. 
So this will work if i would write the parameter as follows:
```
70:
description: Threshold within the retirement income is not taxable for people whom has an age equal to 70  
unit: currency
  values:
    2017-01-01:
      value: 8000.00
71:
  description: Threshold within the retirement income is not taxable for people whom has an age equal to 71
  unit: currency
  values:
    2017-01-01:
      value: 7500.00
``` 

### Here is data (or links to it) that can help you reproduce this issue:
This is the test i wrote to use both formula and parameter :
``` 
- name: Test to see the threshold for people based on their age
  period: 2017-01
  input_variables:
    age: 70
  output_variables:
    threshold_retirement_income_not_taxable: 8000
``` 
### Suggestion
I suggest to implement this functionality because some Italian parameters assume values if the variables are smaller or greater than a certain value.

issue_title: Should get_or_create only work with Data objects which status is Data.STATUS_DONE?
issue_body:
Currently, if processing of a `Data` object fails and the `Data` object has status  `Data.STATUS_ERROR`, it is perfectly valid when a [new `Data` object is created with the `get_or_create` keyword argument](https://github.com/genialis/resolwe/blob/edb2dfec900b6eee42bc11b238201b0cb41b979c/resolwe/flow/views/data.py#L75-L94).

In practice, this means if a `Data` object's processing failed due to an external resource issue, users will constantly receive the same *broken* `Data` object when they use `get_or_create`.

issue_body: Wheels can be built for macOS (#841), and the `delocate-wheel` tool is supposed to take `libdynet.dylib` and relink it into the wheel so that the `DYLD_LIBRARY_PATH` workaround (#146) doesn't have to be used. However, when using `delocate-wheel`, even though `libdynet.dylib` is found, it is not relinked into the wheel. The cause for this problem might be that the DyNet Python files are not in a package, but just at the top level of the installation (matthew-brett/delocate#15).

issue_title: DeprecationWarning::import imp module with Python 3.6
issue_body:
The following warning is raised with Python 3.6 while importing pyart:

`pyart/config.py:22: DeprecationWarning: the imp module is deprecated in favour of importlib; see the module's documentation for alternative uses`

By following importlib documentation we can change l:78 of config.py from:

`cfile = imp.load_source('metadata_config', filename)`

to:

```cfile = importlib.util.spec_from_file_location('metadata_config', filename)
module = importlib.util.module_from_spec(cfile)
cfile.loader.exec_module(module)```

And not forgetting `import importlib.util`

It's only for Python 3.4 and newer. I don't know how to check python version for importing module, so that's why I did not commit a change.

This query should return an iterator with various entities. 

Is this a potential bug with gcloud or with AppEngine? @lexsf @vliang63

issue_title: Storage: Expose preconditions via the google-cloud-storage API
issue_body:
It would be very useful if google-cloud-storage exposed a way to set preconditions on upload. I am currently hacking around this by replacing `google.cloud.storage.blob. _MULTIPART_URL_TEMPLATE` and `google.cloud.storage.blob._RESUMABLE_URL_TEMPLATE` with versions that have the precondition I require, e.g.

```python
from google.cloud import storage as gcs

gcs.blob._MULTIPART_URL_TEMPLATE = (
    f'{gcs.blob._MULTIPART_URL_TEMPLATE}&ifGenerationMatch=0'
)
gcs.blob._RESUMABLE_URL_TEMPLATE = (
    f'{gcs.blob._RESUMABLE_URL_TEMPLATE}&ifGenerationMatch=0'
)
```

Luckily I am only uploading blobs that I do not want to exist beforehand. It would be great if there were a way to programmatically do this, but I could not find anything after pouring over the documentation and source code.

I hope you'll implement this in a future version.

Thanks for your time!

issue_title: GetForwardedIps issues on RHEL7
issue_body:
When GetForwardedIps runs _RunIpRoute (effective command: ip route ls table local type local scope host dev eth0 proto 66), the result is 0 or more lines. If there is one or more forwarded ips, they are returned one per line but have "local " prepended to them.  This causes ParseForwardedIps to fail.

That may not be the case on other systems, but a simple solution works for all of them.  Before returning from GetForwardedIps, simply do a search & replace:
`result = re.sub(r'local\s((\d{1,3}\.){3}\d{1,3}(/\d{1,2})?)\s?\n', r'\1\n',result)`

then return as normal.  If there is a "local " tag, it gets removed, if not, nothing changes.
This helped me stop thousands of warnings from filling up /var/log/messages with:
  google-ip-forwarding: WARNING Could not parse IP address: "local".

issue_title: Documentation is misleading
issue_body:
The documentation is a bit misleading, particularly for beginners.  Instructing people to run this command if the file exists:

```
$ rm app/assets/stylesheets/application.css
```

Will delete the existence of that file, along with what it is doing:

```
/*
 * This is a manifest file that'll be compiled into application.css, which will include all the files
 * listed below.
 *
 * Any CSS and SCSS file within this directory, lib/assets/stylesheets, vendor/assets/stylesheets,
 * or vendor/assets/stylesheets of plugins, if any, can be referenced here using a relative path.
 *
 * You're free to add application-wide styles to this file and they'll appear at the top of the
 * compiled file, but it's generally better to create a new file per style scope.
 *
 *= require_self
 *= require_tree .
 */
```

Really what you want to do is rename the file `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css` to `app/assets/stylesheets/application.css.scss` in order to access the full power of SASS, while maintaining the way the asset pipeline compiles assets, rather than eliminating everything entirely.

http://monosnap.com/image/FeYl1CPoBaeuRbXp435ufQGNZjCmgk

We are building a Shopify theme so I am concatenating the bootstrap sass files with my own styles and then sending them up to Shopify to compile them so I am at the mercy of their compiler. I am told that they are using Sass 3.2.6. However I don't know any other details of their setup other than that.

I guess the issue is I don't actually know if it is your converter that is creating whacky sass, or if it's doing it correctly and Shopify's version of Sass is just to old to handle it. But I figured it needed addressing and this was the best place to start.

My work around for now is to roll back to version 3.3.4.

issue_title: We should find out how GFW works
issue_body:
I have two vps, one at Vultr Tokyo, the other at Conoha Tokyo. Two days ago both of them were blocked, and I changed their ip address. Today they were blocked again. There are only about 10 people using my service and all of them are my schoolmates that I know. However, I have a friend who also have a vps at Vultr Tokyo, which has never been blocked. 

I suppose that many people are suffering from this situation and are continually changing their ip as I did. And I guess the reason of the problem is that GFW is able to label an ip as a SS server after a period collecting traffic data. So I suggest that, in addition to just changing ip, we should discuss how GFW works, (maybe also plan and carry out experiments), to improve the protocol.

issue_title: elasticloadbalancingv2 LoadBalancer validation bug.
issue_body:
I encountered a glitch with the validation of the  LoadBalancer class for elasticloadbalancingv2.  The error occurred when attempting to allow for the creation of either aninternal or internet-facing NLB from the same template.  When trying to generate the template, I received an error instead:

`ValueError: LoadBalancer: only one of the following can be specified: SubnetMappings, Subnets`

The NLB definition causing the error is:

```
    NetworkLB = template.add_resource(elb.LoadBalancer(
        "NetworkLB",
        Name="NetworkLB",
        Scheme="internet-facing",
        Subnets=If(
            "isInternal", 
            [Ref(subnetA), Ref(subnetB)], 
            Ref(AWS_NO_VALUE)),
        SubnetMappings=If("isInternal", Ref(AWS_NO_VALUE), [
            elb.SubnetMapping(
                AllocationId=GetAtt(eipA, 'AllocationId'),
                SubnetId=Ref(subnetA)
            ),
            elb.SubnetMapping(
                AllocationId=GetAtt(eipB, 'AllocationId'),
                SubnetId=Ref(subnetB)
            )
        ]),
        Type='network'
    ))
```
Since this is a valid template, I propose we remove the [validation](https://github.com/cloudtools/troposphere/blob/master/troposphere/elasticloadbalancingv2.py#L143-L148) from this class, and leave it to the template creator to ensure they are not setting the properties incorrectly.  I will also get a PR submitted shortly.

issue_title: later versions of setuptools add new wheel finders
issue_body:
yay!  this may obviate the need for pex/finders.py, except now the following breaks:
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pex/blob/85ef56ad/pex/util.py#L93

the reason: now both the monkeypatched finder provided by pex and the new setuptools finder find a distribution in the pex cache, thus len(distributions) == 2 even though the distributions are identical.  for whatever reason, pex throws up its hands and returns None instead, causing PEXEnvironment.update_candidate_distributions to barf.

i have no idea why the code was written the way it was (senility has crept in), but if you replace list() with set() in order to dedupe and return distributions.pop(), things work regardless of setuptools version.

issue_title: fs_util transitive deps appear to float
issue_body:
@benjy noticed the following failure in master [here](https://travis-ci.org/pantsbuild/pants/jobs/325278629#L2457):

```
[=== 04:18 Building fs_util binary ===]
info: using existing install for '1.21.0-x86_64-apple-darwin'
info: override toolchain for '/Users/travis/build/pantsbuild/pants' set to '1.21.0-x86_64-apple-darwin'
  1.21.0-x86_64-apple-darwin unchanged - rustc 1.21.0 (3b72af97e 2017-10-09)
[Waiting for /Users/travis/.cache/pants/rust/cargo/bin/cargo fetch --manifest-path /Users/travis/build/pantsbuild/pants/src/rust/engine/Cargo.toml (pid 11300) to complete]
    Updating registry `https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index`
    Updating git repository `https://github.com/illicitonion/grpc-rs`
 Downloading crossbeam v0.2.11
   Compiling crossbeam v0.2.11
   Compiling nodrop v0.1.12
error: expected one of `extern` or `fn`, found `auto`
  --> /Users/travis/.cache/pants/rust/cargo/registry/src/github.com-1ecc6299db9ec823/crossbeam-0.2.11/src/mem/cache_padded.rs:54:12
   |
54 | pub unsafe auto trait ZerosValid {}
   |           -^^^^ unexpected token
   |           |
   |           expected one of `extern` or `fn` here
error: aborting due to previous error
error: Could not compile `crossbeam`.
warning: build failed, waiting for other jobs to finish...
error: build failed
cp: /Users/travis/build/pantsbuild/pants/src/rust/engine/fs/fs_util/target/release/fs_util: No such file or directory
Failed to build fs_util
Dry run release failed.
```

which points to a compilation problem with `crossbeam` `0.2.11` - a version not declared for use anywhere but presumably a transitive dep. a new rust compiler version was recently released (1.23) which this version of crossbeam presumably depends on - but we're pinned to 1.20.

in the engine, a Cargo.lock pins `crossbeam` to `0.2.10` which presumably works with our pinned rust toolchain @ 1.2.0:

```
$ rg crossbeam
Cargo.lock
83:name = "crossbeam"
242: "crossbeam 0.2.10 (registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index)",
519:"checksum crossbeam 0.2.10 (registry+https://github.com/rust-lang/crates.io-index)" = "0c5ea215664ca264da8a9d9c3be80d2eaf30923c259d03e870388eb927508f97"
```

so fwict either the `crossbeam` transitive dep should be declared - or a `Cargo.lock` used to prevent this from recurring.

issue_title: Built-in help for all options that take an argument is wrong
issue_body:
As discussed in #1022, the built-in help for all pants options that take an argument is wrong. Example:

```
[drift pants (master)]$ ./pants -h |grep -A 2 logdir
-d <dir>, --logdir <dir>
                        Write logs to files under this directory. (default:
                        None)
```

Specifying `-d/foo/bar/baz` or `-d=/foo/bar/baz` work, specifying `-d /foo/bar/baz` does not. Examining the code, it seems like pants relies on the argparse defaults for printing the help. Unfortunately, pants behavior has diverged from the argparse defaults.

issue_title: Depending on python_tests should either be supported or fail fast.
issue_body:
It's currently possible to declare a dependency on a `python_tests` target, however no dependencies from that target will actually be pulled in, causing the code in question to die at runtime with an `ImportError`. pants should either fail fast when attempting to depend on a `python_tests` target or properly support these types of targets as a dependency.

References to relevant code courtesy of @jsirois:

https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/python/python_chroot.py#L198
https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/master/src/python/pants/backend/python/python_chroot.py#L183

issue_title: Per target test invocation aka "test invocation isolation"
issue_body: Currently, we take all the targets in context and then do a jvm invoke - this needs to be isolated into a per target invocation so that we can break down the work involved in a test run. This would be the stepping stone both for a) test caching and for b) remoting eventually.

issue_title: Render "the last error from pantsd" on disconnection
issue_body:
When a client receives an abrupt disconnection error from pantsd from pantsd, we should attempt to render "the last error" that pantsd recorded.

These types of errors are rendered when, after a connection has been made to pantsd, an error occurs server side. The server-side error is generally rendered as an exception in the `pantsd.log`, but because we've lost the connection, the error is not-always/never rendered over nailgun to the client.

    chunk_type, payload = cls.read_chunk(sock, return_bytes)

    raise cls.TruncatedHeaderError('Failed to read nailgun chunk header ({!r}).'.format(e))

Exception message: abruptly lost active connection to pantsd runner: NailgunError(u'Problem talking to nailgun server (address: 127.0.0.1:37585, remote_pid: -337449): TruncatedHeaderError(u"Failed to read nailgun chunk header (TruncatedRead(u\'Expected 5 bytes before socket shutdown, instead received 0\',)).",)',)

-----

While there are more elegant approaches involving structured logging of exceptions, it's unlikely that those would work significantly better than the simple approach, and would involve incorporating the structured/exception logging in a bunch of locations (and possibly missing some of them).

issue_title: Allow looking up buildcache hosts in pants
issue_body:
This is to propose an implementation to the problem described in [1]. 

In short, today buildcache urls are statically configured in pants. In Twitter case we use a centralized load balancer, that becomes a bottleneck with the recent monobuild launch. Deploying a local proxy is a possibility but not quite ready yet because we have heterogeneous deployment environments besides aurora, jenkins for example.

Proposal here is to add options *resolve-read-from* and *resolve-write-to* to [CacheSetup] (https://github.com/pantsbuild/pants/blob/347e68a37421aa2282c31a07559dcb28b7e52e64/src/python/pants/cache/cache_setup.py), each of which may point to an external http endpoint that supplies buildcache urls.

pseudo code looks something like this:
```
read_from_urls = list(self.options.read_from)
if self.options.resolve_read_from:
    read_from_urls.extend(resolver.resolve(self.options.resolve_read_from))

// the rest logic remain unchanged
best_url = select_best_url(pinger, read_from_urls)
...
```

Ideally the resolved urls are updated periodically to keep them fresh during the lifespan of pants run, but this is something I feel can leave as a future improvement.

[1] [pants-devel discussion](https://groups.google.com/forum/#!topic/pants-devel/LL6jICXSniU).

issue_title: [Pants Tooling] Build file refactor: renaming targets
Implement the `resolve` goal for npm packages.
issue_body: The central requirement for several node tasks will be resolution of package dependencies, aka `npm install`.  The node backend needs a task that can ensure dependencies are resolved and available on the NODE_PATH.  They need not live in the standard node_modules location and could be resolved under .pants.d/.

issue_title: Initialize embedding weights with pretrained word representations
issue_body:
In order to initialize Sockeye embedding weights with pretrained word representations, I am thinking to:
1. Pretrain word representations using Corpus-A and get `Embed-A` and `Vocab-A`.
2. Create the vocabulary of Corpus-B which will be used for NMT, i.e. `Vocab-B`. (Using sockeye-vocab)
3. `Embed-A`, `Vocab-A`, `Vocab-B` -> `Embed-B`.
3.1. Load `Embed-A` and `Vocab-A`;
3.2. Create `Embed-B` with random values;
3.3. Copy word representations from `Embed-A` to `Embed-B` for overlaps between `Vocab-A` and `Vocab-B`.
3.4. Store `Embed-B` in the format of `Dict[str, mx.nd.NDArray]`, str can be for example "target_embed_weight".
4. Start Sockeye training by loading `Embed-B` with model parameter `--params`.

Is this workflow correct? If so I can create a tool to do Step 3.

Some messages may be formatted inconsistently or not logged at all.

issue_body:
The etree walker with implementation `lxml.etree` doesn't work when passed a full html document (having type `lxml.etree._ElementTree`). To reproduce-- def serialize(element, treebuilder, implementation=None): walker_cls = html5lib.getTreeWalker(treebuilder, implementation=implementation) walker = walker_cls(element) serializer = HTMLSerializer(omit_optional_tags=False) html = serializer.render(walker) print(html) html = """ foo

a

b

""" builder = html5lib.getTreeBuilder('lxml') parser = html5lib.HTMLParser(builder, namespaceHTMLElements=False) element = parser.parse(html) serialize(element, 'lxml') serialize(element, 'etree', implementation=lxml.etree) The last line fails with the following error: Traceback (most recent call last): File "test-html5lib.py", line 98, in parse_and_serialize(element, 'etree', implementation=lxml.etree) File "test-html5lib.py", line 79, in serialize html = serializer.render(walker) File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 323, in render return "".join(list(self.serialize(treewalker))) File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/serializer.py", line 209, in serialize for token in treewalker: File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/treewalkers/base.py", line 128, in __iter__ firstChild = self.getFirstChild(currentNode) File "/.../python3.6/site-packages/html5lib/treewalkers/etree.py", line 88, in getFirstChild if element.text: AttributeError: 'lxml.etree._ElementTree' object has no attribute 'text' The walker should probably first be calling `root = element.getroot()` first. This seems to be on the same wave length as the issue with `treewalkers/etree.py` I described in this comment: https://github.com/html5lib/html5lib-python/issues/338#issuecomment-298689916

issue_title: Confidant strips newlines when submitting credentials through webpage
issue_body:
We're using Confidant to store, among other things, our certificates. When making requests between our machines we noticed that they were failing due to a SSL certificate verification error. Through some investigation, I found that the md5sum of the certificate is different after it's been sent through Confidant. 

It seems like this is caused by the angular.js frontend, which I believe is stripping off newlines. I tried pasting in a value with a newline at the end, confirmed that the blank line was there after pasting, and found it to be missing when editing the credential again after saving.

I then tried to edit the value by adding newlines and noticed that the textarea class is still marked as "ng-pristine". After adding alphabetical characters, you can see that "ng-pristine" is removed and "ng-dirty" is added to the textarea class. I've pasted images below showing the three states, where the textarea has not been edited, a newline has been added but the area is still marked as pristine, and then with text added and the area marked as dirty. 

For reference, we're running Confidant version 1.1.13.
